The only explicit, publicly stated monthly amount on the official BZH domain is in a BZH-hosted reprint of a FAZ article dated 26.10.2022 / posted 31.10.2022, stating: 'Wer die Schule besucht, muss monatlich 350 Euro zahlen.' No grade/level differentiation, billing-month count, enrollment/registration fee, income definition, or sibling discounts are stated alongside this fee. Therefore, monthly tuition is treated as a flat 350 EUR per month for all provided household income points (not income-based).
tuition_tier_reasoning: According to official documents from the Hessian State Parliament regarding the Bildungszentrum Hermann Hesse (BZH) in Frankfurt, it is a state-recognized private special school (Förderschule) geared toward adolescents and young adults dealing with addiction or other social problems. description_en: Bürgermeister-Grimm-Schule is a state-run special needs school in Frankfurt am Main, specializing in supporting students with learning challenges. Located in the Gallus neighborhood, the school serves approximately 140 students, providing individualized education in a nurturing environment. The school emphasizes personalized support through experienced special needs teachers across three main stages: Grundstufe, Mittelstufe, and Berufsorientierungsstufe. Students are encouraged to learn at their own pace while building on their strengths and developing social skills. A key feature is the comprehensive all-day program, which includes a variety of extracurricular activities such as sports, music, and art. Additionally, students benefit from specialized therapies such as occupational therapy and speech therapy provided in collaboration with external partners. The school also actively participates as a project school to support the integration of young migrants. Bürgermeister-Grimm-Schule offers a safe and supportive learning environment, making it an ideal choice for families seeking an inclusive educational setting.

description_en: The Charles-Hallgarten-Schule is a public special needs school located in Frankfurt am Main, specializing in support for students with learning difficulties. Situated in the Bornheim neighborhood, the school caters to approximately 110 to 120 students from grades 1 to 10. It emphasizes individualized support to help students overcome learning barriers and develop their personal strengths. As a full-day school, Charles-Hallgarten-Schule not only provides academic assistance but also offers practical projects and vocational orientation aimed at successful reintegration into mainstream schools or transition to vocational training. A key feature of the school is its regional counseling and support center, which provides guidance and support for children and youth with significant learning impairments. The school also collaborates closely with external partners and offers a variety of afternoon programs along with language support in English, Turkish, and other languages. With small class sizes and a dedicated staff, Charles-Hallgarten-Schule fosters a nurturing and inclusive learning environment.

description: Fritz-Redl-Schule ist eine Förderschule in Frankfurt am Main. Adresse: Kurmainzer Straße 20, 65929 Frankfurt am Main. Mittlere ÖPNV-Anbindung. Erhöhtes Verkehrsunfallaufkommen (87/Jahr).
description_en:
Fritz-Redl-Schule is a special needs school located in the Höchst district of Frankfurt am Main, dedicated to supporting students with emotional and social developmental needs. Established in 2015, the school currently accommodates around 40 students organized into five small learning groups, with a maximum of eight students per class. This structure allows for intensive, individualized support tailored to each student's unique needs. Instead of traditional grade-based classes, the school employs mixed-age learning groups, fostering a flexible and personalized educational experience.

The school is conveniently situated near Höchster Stadtpark, and it features accessible facilities to ensure inclusivity. Since 2023, Fritz-Redl-Schule has partnered with the Evangelical Association for Youth Social Work to offer a comprehensive program that provides counseling and social learning opportunities for students. Additional services include career orientation programs, violence prevention initiatives, and English language instruction.

By maintaining strong connections with local employment agencies, the school facilitates smooth transitions for students into the workforce and independent living, with a designated contact person from the Federal Employment Agency coordinating vocational guidance. Overall, Fritz-Redl-Schule is a vital part of the support network for special educational needs in Frankfurt, providing both a nurturing learning environment and essential resources for its students.

description: Heinrich-Hoffmann-Schule ist eine Förderschule in Frankfurt am Main. Adresse: Marienburgstraße 4, 60528 Frankfurt am Main. Mittlere ÖPNV-Anbindung. Erhöhtes Verkehrsunfallaufkommen (87/Jahr).
description_en: Heinrich-Hoffmann-Schule is a public special needs school in Frankfurt am Main that specializes in supporting chronically ill students. As a Special Education Advisory and Support Center, the school provides tailored educational offerings for children and youth of all school types, particularly those whose health challenges impact their learning. The school ensures that students can keep pace with their studies even during extended hospital stays, addressing their unique educational needs. A key feature of Heinrich-Hoffmann-Schule is its role as a regional advisory center, providing expert guidance to parents and teachers. This includes specialized counseling on the autism spectrum and assistance in developing individualized education plans. The school also offers a variety of language programs, including English, French, Latin, and Spanish, enabling students to engage in diverse curricula despite health limitations. With approximately 234 students enrolled, the school emphasizes continuous professional development for its staff. Parents appreciate the user-friendly structure and comprehensive advisory services, which streamline access to support. Located in Sachsenhausen, the proximity to medical facilities enhances the school's mission of integrating education and healthcare. Overall, Heinrich-Hoffmann-Schule offers holistic support that empowers students to thrive academically and socially.
foerderverein: "Verein der Freunde und Förderer der Schule für Kranke für Kranke e.V.", Ansprechpartner/in: Frau Birgit Neitzel-Gehrig
namensgebung: Heinrich Hoffmann (1809-1894), angesehener Frankfurter Bürger, der als Arzt die Psychiatrie wegweisend reformiert hat und durch sein Kinderbuch "Der Struwwelpeter" weltweit bekannt wurde.
schulform_bemerkung: Die Heinrich-Hoffmann-Schule beschult Schülerinnen und Schüler, die aufgrund einer ärztlich festgestellten physischen, psychischen oder somatischen Beeinträchtigung nicht am Unterricht der Stammschule teilnehmen können.

description_en: The Hermann-Herzog-Schule (HHS) is a specialized school in Frankfurt am Main dedicated to supporting students with visual impairments or blindness. Located in the Gutleutviertel neighborhood, the school serves children from grades 1 to 10, providing tailored educational experiences for approximately 94 students in a nurturing environment. With a dedicated staff of around 40 members, including teachers and educational specialists, HHS emphasizes individualized learning and strong collaboration with families. The school operates an overregional advisory and support center (üBFZ), offering assistance to mainstream schools in the area. The curriculum is enriched with specialized programs, including IT basics, life skills training, and swimming lessons, with English taught as the first foreign language. HHS values parent involvement and fosters a holistic development approach, ensuring each child's potential is recognized and nurtured. With its commitment to inclusivity and personalized education, the Hermann-Herzog-Schule stands out as a trusted option for families navigating the challenges of visual impairments.

description_en: The Hermann-Luppe-Schule is a public special needs school in Frankfurt am Main, focusing on supporting students with emotional and social development challenges. Located in the Praunheim neighborhood, it serves as a through-school for children from first to seventh grade who require specialized assistance due to emotional disturbances or social behavior issues. The school's mission is to facilitate reintegration into mainstream education while providing individualized support tailored to students' needs. A standout feature is the integrated youth welfare program, launching in February 2024, which offers a variety of activities and counseling hours for students. The curriculum aligns with Hessian educational standards, emphasizing a structured learning environment that prepares students for transitions to regular schools. With approximately 60 students enrolled, the small class sizes allow for personalized attention. The school's parents' association plays a vital role in funding projects and resources, promoting community involvement. The Hermann-Luppe-Schule offers a compassionate and supportive educational experience, making it an ideal choice for families seeking a nurturing environment for children needing additional emotional support.

description: Johann-Hinrich-Wichern-Schule ist eine Förderschule in Frankfurt am Main. Adresse: Victor-Gollancz-Weg 4, 60433 Frankfurt am Main. Mittlere ÖPNV-Anbindung. Erhöhtes Verkehrsunfallaufkommen (87/Jahr).
description_en:
The Johann-Hinrich-Wichern-Schule is a public special needs school located in Frankfurt am Main, specifically focused on supporting students with learning challenges. Situated in the Eschersheim neighborhood, the school also serves as a Regional Advisory and Support Center (rBFZ) for the northern and central districts, providing comprehensive educational and advisory services for children with special needs. With a rich history dating back to 1963, the school has transformed into a modern center for inclusive education, dedicated to offering individualized support for students with diverse impairments.

The school features a well-structured curriculum tailored to students with learning disabilities, with a key highlight being its “School with Competence and Practical Experience” profile, which prepares students for real-world transitions. It collaborates closely with the IGS Eschersheim, allowing students to earn a Hauptschulabschluss (lower secondary school certificate) and facilitating pathways to a Realschulabschluss (intermediate secondary certificate) through vocational schools.

Beyond academics, the school provides a rich array of programs aimed at nurturing the whole child, including career orientation, enhanced reading support, German language assistance, and all-day programs. The emphasis on a positive school environment ensures that learning challenges are met with practical and holistic strategies. With strong partnerships and a dedicated support association, the Johann-Hinrich-Wichern-Schule creates a welcoming, child-centered atmosphere where students are encouraged to thrive.

Parents will find a structured yet flexible educational setting that helps their children thrive in a diverse urban environment.

description_en: Michael-Schule is a private Waldorf special needs school located in the Griesheim neighborhood of Frankfurt. Dedicated to providing individualized support for children and youth requiring special educational assistance, the school serves up to 95 students from preschool through the 12th grade. It fosters personal growth within a close-knit community based on the anthroposophical curative education principles of Rudolf Steiner. The school focuses on holistic education, addressing not only learning challenges but also emotional and social development, and intellectual growth. Students learn together in stable class communities, where the diversity among peers is seen as a strength for social and learning maturation. The curriculum emphasizes practical and artistic elements, allowing children to engage in creative activities and therapies that support their developmental needs. A dedicated team of teachers and helpers ensures comprehensive care, creating a nurturing environment. For families seeking a supportive and tailored educational experience in Frankfurt, Michael-Schule offers a unique path to help children thrive.

description_en:
The Monikahausschule is a private, state-recognized special needs school located in the Gallus neighborhood of Frankfurt, serving approximately 27 students from grades 1 to 6. Operated by the Sozialdienst katholischer Frauen e.V. (SkF) within the Monikahaus Family Center, the school focuses on the social-emotional development of children with special needs. With small, mixed-age learning groups, the Monikahausschule fosters a family-like atmosphere that emphasizes trust and mutual respect.

The school aims to create positive learning experiences and prepare students for reintegration into mainstream education. Its flexible learning environment promotes self-discovery and social engagement, allowing children to build resilience. A multidisciplinary team provides supervision until 6 PM, offering homework help, creative projects, and various activities. Parental involvement is highly valued, with counseling and support programs enhancing the community aspect. The Monikahausschule celebrates diversity and creates a safe space for emotional growth. For the 2024/25 school year, there are 27 spots available, ensuring personalized attention and high educational quality. Families looking for a nurturing environment will find the Monikahausschule to be an excellent choice for their children's development.

description_en: Panoramaschule is a public special needs school located in Frankfurt am Main, specializing in support for students with intellectual disabilities. Situated at Werner-Bockelmann-Straße 3 in the Nied neighborhood, the school provides individualized education, care, and therapy for children and youth of compulsory school age, regardless of the severity of their disability. Established in the 2011/2012 school year, Panoramaschule's educational mission, "Self-realization in social integration," guides its comprehensive approach to learning. The school emphasizes practical, life-oriented education, integrating teaching, upbringing, and therapy to foster independence and social skills. As a fully bound all-day school, the daily schedule includes shared breaks, a hot lunch, and afternoon activities such as psychomotorics, dance, swimming, and soccer. The dedicated staff, including special education teachers and trained therapists, ensures that all students receive tailored support. Additionally, the school is backed by a committed parent association that enhances educational conditions. Parents value the Panoramaschule's inclusive and practical approach, which promotes academic growth and prepares students for real-world challenges in a nurturing community.
foerderverein: Förderverein Panoramaschule e. V.: förderverein@panoramaschule-frankfurt.de
namensgebung: Das Wort "Panorama" bedeutet wörtlich übersetzt „alles sehen“ oder auch „das Ganze sehen“, was wiederum ein ganzheitliches Menschenbild impliziert. Nicht die einzelne Behinderung, sondern der Mensch als Ganzes mit allen seinen Fähigkeiten und Fertigkeiten soll und muss geachtet werden. Darüber hinaus bietet das Schulgebäude der Panoramaschule in Frankfurt Nied einen wunderschönen Panoramablick auf die Skyline der Stadt Frankfurt und auf den Taunus.

description_en:
The Schule im Reinhardshof is a state-recognized special needs school located in Frankfurt am Main, Germany. It specializes in emotional and social development, providing a nurturing environment for students facing challenges. Situated in the Nieder-Erlenbach neighborhood, the school operates under the auspices of the evangelical youth welfare organization Kinderheimat Reinhardshof. With a maximum enrollment of 23 students aged 10 to 17, the school focuses on individualized support for those who are temporarily unable to attend mainstream education.

Classes are intentionally small, with 6 to 8 students per group, allowing for personalized attention and flexible scheduling tailored to each child's needs. In addition to core subjects, English is offered, and the school prepares students for the Hauptschulabschluss, the lower secondary school certificate. The facilities are designed to support a holistic educational approach, featuring well-equipped classrooms, a library, a workshop, a gymnasium, and a school garden, all of which enhance both learning and leisure activities.

A standout aspect of Schule im Reinhardshof is its all-day care program, where students transition into mixed-age groups after classes. This program maintains a low supervision ratio, providing a safe and supportive environment that fosters independence and life skills. With its compassionate approach and structured environment, the Schule im Reinhardshof is an excellent choice for families seeking comprehensive support for their children’s emotional, social, and educational needs.

description_en: The Viktor-Frankl-Schule is a public special needs school in Frankfurt am Main, dedicated to supporting students with physical and motor development challenges. Located in the Dornbusch neighborhood, the school provides a nurturing environment tailored to the individual needs of children and youth. In addition to an individualized educational approach that emphasizes motor skills and life skills, the school offers afternoon activity groups, vocational orientation programs, and comprehensive support services for students. Starting in the 2024/25 school year, a new branch will cater to students requiring intellectual development support. The school collaborates with local partners to enrich student experiences and offers English as a language option. A strong focus on career preparation is evident, with on-site advisors from the Federal Employment Agency providing consultations. As a vital regional advisory and support center, the school assists Frankfurt's general schools in integrating students with special needs through inclusive strategies and individualized support plans. Families appreciate the school’s holistic approach to development, which fosters a strong sense of community and extends beyond academic support.
